concepts.name: Binary Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree data structure where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left child and the right child.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental building block of a tree, containing data and references to its children.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a binary tree, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Leaf Node
concepts.short_definition: A node in a binary tree that has no children.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has one or more children.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that is directly connected to another node (its parent) and descends from it.

concepts.name: Height
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges on the longest path from the root node to a leaf node.

concepts.name: Depth
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges from the root node to a specific node.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a binary tree in a systematic order.

concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in its left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in its right subtree are greater.
